​@ohwell2790,  Born in 1963. When I was in school:

Teachers were expected to:
1.) Dress professionally.
2.) Leave their politics at home. The execption being in Civics Class in a debate about an issue between students. The teacher would ask questions for both sides, but we never knew his politics.
3.) To leave their sex life at home.
4.) To teach students how to research, use critical thinking skills, think for themselves and ask questions.
5.) To teach the students the subjects they needed in life, the job market and finances.
6.) Never invite students to your house.

The students were expected to:
1.) Address the teachers by Mr., Miss, Mrs.
2.) Learn the subjects.
3.) Be mannered and polite.
4.) Do the work assignments.
5.) Be on time.
6.) Never go to a teachers house.

Everyday when I got home from school, mom's first question was if I had homework. If I did, then I sat at the kitchen table until it was done. I had teachers stay after school to help with subjects I had any problems with. If there was a problem student, off to the principle for a paddling. Soon as the principle was done with the paddling and sent back to class, the principle was on the phone with mom or dad and told them he paddled and why. Then when you got home, expect a spanking.

I think a lot of the problems with kids today is because of the left. Remember in the 1990's when the left went on their nationwide spanking is child abuse campaign? They were telling kids if they were spanked, to call the police and have the parent arrested for child abuse. So parents stopped spanking for fear of arrest. Then what happened? The mass shootings started with Columbine by kids who were growing up and told spanking was child abuse.
